findings.issue: Font sizes and styles may not be consistently applied across the interface, leading to a disjointed visual experience.
findings.impact: Reduced readability and a less professional look and feel.
findings.recommendation: Establish a clear typography hierarchy with defined font sizes, weights, and styles for headings, body text, and other elements.

findings.issue: Insufficient whitespace around elements can make the interface feel cluttered and difficult to scan.
findings.impact: Reduced readability and difficulty in distinguishing between different sections of the page.
findings.recommendation: Increase whitespace around elements to improve readability and create a more visually appealing layout.

findings.issue: Lack of explicit labels or ARIA attributes for interactive elements may hinder screen reader users from understanding their purpose and function. e.g., filters
findings.impact: Difficult for screen reader users to navigate and interact with the interface.
findings.recommendation: Provide explicit labels or ARIA attributes for all interactive elements to ensure accessibility for screen reader users.

findings.issue: Insufficient color contrast between text and background may make it difficult for users with visual impairments to read the content.
findings.impact: Reduced readability for users with low vision or color blindness.
findings.recommendation: Ensure sufficient color contrast between text and background to meet WCAG guidelines.

findings.issue: The 'Loading tasks...' message provides minimal feedback and doesn't indicate progress.
findings.impact: Users may experience uncertainty and frustration while waiting for tasks to load.
findings.recommendation: Implement a more informative loading indicator, such as a progress bar or spinner, to provide feedback on the loading process.

findings.issue: Using standard select boxes/dropdowns might not be the optimal experience when many categories/priorities exist. User might need to scroll through a long list.
findings.recommendation: Consider implementing a searchable select component or a multi-select component with a filter option for improved usability.

findings.issue: The layout and navigation may not adapt well to smaller screen sizes, potentially leading to horizontal scrolling or content being cut off.
findings.impact: Difficult navigation and usability issues on mobile and tablet devices.
findings.recommendation: Implement a responsive design that adapts the layout and navigation to different screen sizes using CSS media queries.

strengths: Basic task management features are present.

improvement_priorities.reason: Ensuring proper labels and ARIA attributes is crucial for users with disabilities to effectively use the application.
A responsive design is essential for providing a consistent user experience across different devices.
Improving the visual design can enhance the user experience and create a more professional look and feel.
